name: Alphabet Inc.
shortDescription: Google's parent: the advertising franchise (Search, YouTube) plus a fast-accelerating Google Cloud and full-stack AI (Gemini + custom TPUs).
businessModel: Three engines. (1) Advertising — Search & other ($60.4B Q1'26, +19%), YouTube ads ($9.88B), and Google Network, on auction/CPC/CPM economics; the cash core. (2) Google Cloud ($20.0B, +63%) — consumption GCP infra/platform plus Workspace, now led by enterprise AI infrastructure (TPU/GPU) and AI solutions (Gemini Enterprise, Vertex). (3) Subscriptions, platforms & devices ($12.4B) — YouTube Premium/TV, Google One, Play, Pixel (350M paid subs). Gemini is monetized via API (>16B tokens/min) and embedded across surfaces.
marketContext: Alphabet sits at the convergence of digital advertising (mature, defending Search against AI-native disruption and DOJ), cloud (hyper-growth, #3 hyperscaler closing on AWS/Azure via differentiated TPU economics), and frontier AI (Gemini 3 re-rated Google as a co-leader vs OpenAI/Anthropic). Tailwinds: AI Overviews/AI Mode lifting query volume, >$460B Cloud backlog, TPU emerging as a sellable Nvidia alternative (Anthropic ~$40B/5GW, Meta talks, Blackstone neocloud JV), and Apple basing next-gen Siri on Gemini. Headwinds: ~$180-190B 2026 capex, twin antitrust overhangs (Search appeal + adtech divestiture risk).
competitors: Nvidia (AI compute; TPU is the alternative)
customers: Enterprises/developers on Google Cloud, Vertex, Gemini Enterprise
AI labs buying TPU capacity (Anthropic, reportedly Meta)
Consumers (Search, Android, Chrome, YouTube; 750M+ Gemini MAUs)

leadership.name: Sundar Pichai
leadership.title: CEO, Alphabet and Google
leadership.background: Joined Google in 2004; led Chrome and Android before becoming Google CEO (2015) and Alphabet CEO (2019). Architect of the full-stack AI strategy.

leadership.name: Anat Ashkenazi
leadership.title: CFO & SVP, Alphabet and Google
leadership.background: Joined Alphabet as CFO in 2024 from Eli Lilly (CFO). Oversees the record AI capex program ($180-190B guided for 2026).

leadership.name: Ruth Porat
leadership.title: President & Chief Investment Officer
leadership.background: Former Morgan Stanley CFO; Alphabet CFO 2015-2024 before transitioning to President & CIO.

notes: Trading multiples pulled live via yfinance as of the 2026-06-03 market close (EV/Rev recomputed from close x shares + net debt). All comps share that single close anchor. Qualitative facts sourced to Alphabet IR/SEC, CNBC, Bloomberg, Fortune, and the Q1 2026 release/transcript.

businessOverview: Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOGL) is the holding company for Google plus 'Other Bets' moonshots, valued at ~$2.1T (2026-06-03 close).
Q1 2026 revenue was $109.9B (+22% YoY) — an 11th straight quarter of double-digit growth.
Google Services: $89.6B (+16%) — Search & other $60.4B (+19%), YouTube ads $9.88B, subscriptions/platforms/devices $12.4B.
Google Cloud: $20.0B (+63%), with enterprise AI solutions the primary Cloud growth driver for the first time.
Operating margin expanded to 36.1%; net income $62.6B (+81%); Cloud backlog/RPO topped $460B.
Full-stack AI thesis: Gemini frontier models + custom TPU silicon + global data centers + distribution across Search/Android/Workspace/Cloud.
TPU is becoming a sellable Nvidia alternative: Anthropic up to ~$40B/5GW, Meta in talks, a Blackstone neocloud JV.
2026 capex guided to $180-190B; twin antitrust overhangs (DOJ Search appeal + adtech remedy) persist.
brief.productModel: Advertising is the cash core (Search $60.4B +19%, YouTube ads $9.88B, Network), sold on auction/CPC/CPM economics. Google Cloud ($20.0B, +63%) monetizes consumption GCP infrastructure/platform plus Workspace SaaS, increasingly driven by enterprise AI infra (TPU/GPU compute) and AI solutions (Gemini Enterprise, Vertex AI). Subscriptions/platforms/devices ($12.4B) covers YouTube Premium/TV, Google One, Play take-rate, and Pixel. Gemini is monetized directly via API (>16B tokens/minute, +60% QoQ) and embedded across consumer and enterprise surfaces; serving unit costs fell ~78% over 2025.

recentNews.headline: Alphabet Q1 2026: revenue +22% to $109.9B, Google Cloud surges 63% to $20.0B
recentNews.whyItMatters: 11th straight quarter of double-digit growth; Cloud acceleration and 36.1% operating margin signal AI investment is converting to revenue. Cloud backlog topped $460B.

recentNews.headline: Alphabet raises 2026 capex guidance to $180-190B
recentNews.whyItMatters: Up from $175-185B and more than double 2025 spend, underscoring the scale of the AI infrastructure bet and pressuring near-term free cash flow.

recentNews.headline: At Cloud Next 2026, Google makes Ironwood (7th-gen TPU) GA and previews 8th-gen TPUs
recentNews.whyItMatters: Ironwood targets the 'age of inference' (9,216 chips/superpod); previewed TPU 8t (training, Broadcom) and 8i (inference, MediaTek) on TSMC 2nm cement a multi-year custom-silicon roadmap vs Nvidia.

recentNews.headline: Google and Blackstone to create a TPU-based 'neocloud' service
recentNews.whyItMatters: First hyperscaler to offer its AI chips outside its own cloud, opening a merchant-silicon revenue path and a direct attack on Nvidia's installed base.

recentNews.headline: AI wins have Alphabet poised to become one of the world's most valuable companies
recentNews.whyItMatters: Gemini 3 leadership and Cloud momentum re-rated the stock; Alphabet crossed $4T in January 2026, second only to Nvidia at points in 2026.

recentNews.headline: DeepMind researchers queue for TPUs as Google sells record capacity to Anthropic and Meta
recentNews.whyItMatters: Highlights strong external TPU demand (Anthropic up to ~$40B/5GW; Meta in talks) alongside an internal supply-constraint risk for Google's own frontier training.

recentNews.headline: Google unveils Gemini 3.5 Flash at I/O 2026 for agents and coding
recentNews.whyItMatters: Cheaper, faster model extends Gemini's price/performance lead for agentic and developer workloads, improving AI gross-margin economics.

recentNews.headline: DOJ and 35 states appeal Search remedy; adtech remedies ruling looms
recentNews.whyItMatters: After the judge rejected a forced Chrome divestiture, the appeal seeks tougher structural remedies; the separate adtech case still threatens divestiture of Google's ad-exchange business — a multi-year overhang.

earningsTakeaways.aiCommentary: AI was the dominant narrative. Management framed the 'full-stack' approach — Gemini models, custom TPU silicon, global infrastructure, distribution — as lighting up every part of the business. Enterprise AI solutions became Cloud's primary growth driver for the first time; Gemini Enterprise paid MAUs rose 40% QoQ and Gemini processed >16B tokens/minute via direct API (+60% QoQ). Serving unit costs fell ~78% over 2025. In Search, AI Overviews/AI Mode drove record query volume and 19% revenue growth.
earningsTakeaways.demandCommentary: Demand was broad-based. Cloud accelerated to 63% on enterprise AI infrastructure and solutions, with a >$460B backlog of multi-year committed contracts. Advertising held up with Search at all-time-high query volume and YouTube ads at $9.88B. External TPU demand was a standout (Anthropic ~$40B/5GW; Meta in talks; Blackstone JV). Management flagged compute capacity as a gating factor for both external sales and internal training.

keyRisks: AI-native query disruption: generative answers could erode long-term Search ad clicks even as near-term query volume rises.
Capital intensity: $180-190B 2026 capex more than doubles 2025 and pressures near-term FCF if AI revenue lags.
Antitrust: DOJ/35-state Search appeal seeks tougher remedies; the separate adtech case still threatens an ad-exchange divestiture.
TPU supply is a two-edged sword: selling record external capacity (Anthropic, Meta) leaves DeepMind queuing for its own compute.
Cloud is #3 and must keep closing the gap on AWS/Azure to justify the re-rating.
Other Bets remain a ~$2.1B/quarter operating loss with Waymo still pre-scale.
Valuation: after crossing $4T, the stock prices in continued AI and Cloud execution.

comps.name: Alphabet
comps.note: Cheapest mega-cap on EV/Rev despite the fastest growth ex-Nvidia; ads cash core + 63%-growth Cloud + full-stack AI

comps.name: Microsoft
comps.note: Closest cloud+AI comparable; Azure +40%, at roughly 2x Alphabet's EV/Rev

comps.name: Meta Platforms
comps.note: The other half of the ad duopoly; faster growth and richer margin, trades above Alphabet

comps.name: Amazon
comps.note: AWS is the #1 cloud Alphabet is chasing; lowest EV/Rev of the group on retail mix

comps.name: Apple
comps.note: Largest by market cap; a Gemini customer for next-gen Siri

comps.name: Nvidia
comps.note: Alphabet's TPUs are the in-house alternative to Nvidia GPUs; the AI-cycle multiple anchor

slideBullets: Alphabet Q1'26: revenue $109.9B (+22%), Cloud $20.0B (+63%), op margin 36.1%, net income $62.6B — AI spend is converting to revenue.
Trades at 4.9x EV/Rev (2026-06-03 close), the cheapest of the big-tech mega-caps (MSFT/AAPL ~10x, META 6.4x) despite the fastest growth ex-Nvidia.
Full-stack AI is the differentiator: Gemini 3 leadership, 750M+ Gemini MAUs, and custom TPUs now sold externally (Anthropic ~$40B/5GW, Blackstone JV).
Cloud backlog >$460B and Apple choosing Gemini for Siri validate enterprise/model leadership — but $180-190B capex is the price.
Key debate: do AI Overviews protect Search economics, and do the DOJ Search appeal + adtech remedy stay contained?
diligenceQuestions: How durable is Search ad monetization as AI Mode/AI Overviews shift query behavior — what is revenue-per-query doing?
What is the realistic external-TPU revenue ramp net of cannibalizing DeepMind's own training capacity, and at what gross margin?
Across scenarios for the DOJ Search appeal and the adtech remedy, what is the downside to ad EBIT and group multiple?

rampDemandSignal.headline: Google Ads holds #2 in Paid-Ads card spend (behind Meta), but Google captures only ~4.5% of AI-vendor adoption — Gemini barely registers in the LLM spend race that OpenAI and Anthropic now split ~99% of.
rampDemandSignal.note: Two-sided read: Google Ads is a Paid-Ads spend leader, but Google's share of AI/LLM adoption is a fraction of OpenAI's and Anthropic's. Ramp's panel is corporate-card spend across 50k+ businesses and skews SMB / mid-market, so it under-captures committed enterprise contracts.
